text: William James wrote in 1890 that 'the faculty of voluntarily bringing back a wandering attention, over and over again, is the very root of judgment, character, and will.' He understood attention as a selecting force — consciousness, he argued, is not a passive mirror of the world but an active editor. What we attend to constitutes, in a real sense, what we experience. Fragmented attention therefore does not merely reduce productivity; it fragments the self. A mind perpetually pulled between inputs never fully inhabits any of them, producing a kind of experiential poverty despite apparent busyness.
question: According to William James, what does voluntarily returning a wandering attention represent?

options.text: The root of judgment, character, and will.
A compensatory mechanism for low working memory capacity.
A sign of hyperfocus that can become pathological.
An automatic reflex that cannot be trained or cultivated.
